summary: The selected papers emphasize precision jet-medium theory, charmonium-jet correlations in Pb+Pb, spin-sensitive femtoscopy, baryon-stopping diagnostics, and analysis-grade ML/statistical methods for collider workflows. The strongest heavy-ion papers are the QCD antenna calculation with realistic medium interactions and the ATLAS J/psi-jet correlation measurement. The strongest AI/ML paper is an amplitude-assisted polarization extraction study using NLO QCD plus parton-shower effects and explicit baseline comparisons.
strongest_paper_summary: Gluon radiation from a QCD antenna with realistic parton-medium interactions is the strongest theory/phenomenology item because it directly targets a precision bottleneck in jet-quenching calculations by replacing limited harmonic-oscillator approximations with a numerical all-orders treatment of realistic medium scattering.

papers.title: Gluon radiation from a QCD antenna with realistic parton-medium interactions
papers.authors: Carlota Andres, Liliana Apolinario, Nestor Armesto, Andre Cordeiro, Fabio Dominguez, Pablo Guerrero-Rodriguez, Jose Guilherme Milhano
papers.abstract: The paper expresses the full in-medium gluon emission spectrum from a quark-antiquark antenna as differential equations solvable numerically, resumming medium interactions to all orders with realistic scattering models.

papers.title: Measurement of J/psi-jet correlations in pp and Pb+Pb collisions at sqrt(sNN)=5.02 TeV with the ATLAS detector
papers.abstract: ATLAS measures inclusive, isolated, and non-isolated J/psi production in pp and Pb+Pb, separating prompt and non-prompt components and reporting nuclear modification factors up to pT = 60 GeV.

papers.title: Spin Femtoscopy: A Framework for Revealing Genuine Spin Correlations
papers.authors: Kehao Zhang, Xuan Wang, Xiaofeng Luo
papers.abstract: The paper proposes spin-resolved femtoscopic correlation functions, using Lambda-Lambda pairs as a proof of principle, to isolate genuine two-particle spin correlations from spin-dependent femtoscopic mixing.

papers.title: Putting Jet Substructure on Track(s)
papers.authors: Kyle Lee, Ian Moult, Wouter J. Waalewijn
papers.abstract: The paper computes tracked projected energy correlators up to four points at next-to-leading collinear logarithmic accuracy, extending state-of-the-art jet substructure calculations to track-based observables.

papers.title: Dipolar flow of identified hadrons at mid-rapidity using transport models
papers.authors: Abhisek Praharaj, Aradhana Panday, Sandeep Chatterjee, Md Nasim
papers.abstract: The paper studies rapidity-even dipolar flow for identified hadrons in Au+Au collisions using AMPT, comparing with HIJING to assess non-flow contributions and highlighting proton-antiproton splitting at lower beam energies.

papers.title: Higher-order effects in amplitude-assisted polarisation extraction with machine-learning techniques
papers.authors: Juan M. Cruz-Martinez, Jakob Linder, Mathieu Pellen, Giovanni Pelliccioli, Emanuele Re
papers.abstract: The work presents amplitude-assisted ML regression/classification at NLO QCD with parton-shower effects for extracting longitudinal vector-boson production rates, benchmarking neural architectures against random forests.

papers.title: GAIA: Geometry-Adaptive Operator Learning for Forward and Inverse Problems
papers.authors: Meenakshi Krishnan, Pranav Pulijala, Ke Chen, Haizhao Yang, Ramani Duraiswami
papers.abstract: GAIA uses geometry tokens and cross-attention-conditioned integral layers for forward, boundary-value, and inverse PDE problems on arbitrary domains, reporting strong results on several 2D and 3D benchmarks.

papers.title: Modeling Falling Backgrounds with Exponential Mixtures
papers.authors: Austin Townsend, Marc Osherson, Mike Hildreth, Stefano Castruccio
papers.abstract: The paper proposes finite exponential mixtures as semi-parametric models for falling LHC backgrounds and reports comparable performance to existing methods on published data and simulation studies with small bias and nominal bulk coverage.

papers.title: Shedding light on the nature of phi(2170) with the parton and hadron cascade model PACIAE
papers.authors: Jian Cao, Wen-Chao Zhang, Bo Feng, Ya-Hui Hou, An-Ke Lei, Zhi-Lei She, Hua Zheng, Li-Lin Zhu, Dai-Mei Zhou, Yu-Liang Yan, Ben-Hao Sa
papers.abstract: The paper simulates multiple phi(2170) candidate structures in PACIAE and compares coalescence/recombination yields and spectra across strangeonium, hybrid, tetraquark, molecular, and resonance assignments.
